personas.role: AI coding developer who discovers and installs shared agents into their harness
goals: Find a trusted internal agent or component instead of rebuilding it
Install into the correct harness with one command and keep working
Diagnose when an agent or tool fails using traces or doctor guidance
constraints: Limited patience for multi-harness setup and broken config
Works primarily inside a coding harness (Claude Code, Cursor, Kiro, Pi, etc.)
Prefers approved registry items but may need own pending submissions
evidence: README.md: What is Observal / Why teams use Observal / Quick Start
AGENTS.md: pull as primary workflow; CLI / web / skill interaction modes

personas.role: Internal agent or component author who publishes and maintains shared packages
goals: Package MCP servers, skills, hooks, prompts, and sandboxes into a versioned agent
Get submissions reviewed and adopted across harnesses
Learn from usage and insight reports what to improve or deprecate
constraints: Must satisfy admin review before broad install (except owner fallback)
Must keep versions and harness compatibility coherent
Lacks visibility when usage feedback is missing
evidence: README.md: Agents are portable context packages; registry; insights; review diffs
AGENTS.md: agent create/build/publish; component types; owner-install fallback

personas.role: Platform or org admin who governs the registry and user access
goals: Review, approve, or reject submissions with enough context to decide safely
Keep a trusted catalog and audit trail for internal AI components
Manage users, org settings, and policy without blocking legitimate owners
constraints: Accountable for what becomes installable org-wide
Needs diffs, prompts, and audit signals—not anecdote-only review
Must balance speed-to-publish against safety and compliance expectations
evidence: README.md: Review and Governance; audit logs; SSO/SCIM mentions
AGENTS.md: admin review routes; audit system; auth model

personas.role: Operator who deploys and maintains the self-hosted Observal stack
goals: Stand up and upgrade a reliable Docker-based control plane
Keep auth, databases, ingest, and web healthy
Back up, restore, and diagnose failures without data loss
constraints: Owns Postgres, ClickHouse, Redis, and compose topology complexity
Must follow migration and upgrade docs; Redis fail-closed affects auth
Not the primary registry end user day-to-day
evidence: README.md: Deploy the server; self-hosting docs links
AGENTS.md: Docker stack services; ClickHouse migrations; Redis auth fail-closed
